proposals.reason: MDCG 2021-24 rev.1 published 20 April 2026 with updated classification guidance. Key changes to Rules 2, 8, 9, 10, 12, 16, 22.

card.x: Revised guidance on medical device classification. Key changes: 'surgical' replaced by 'clinical' for invasiveness, updates to Rules 2, 8, 9, 10, 12, 16 & 22 with new examples. No software-specific rule changes in this revision.

proposals.reason: Borderline and Classification Manual v5 published 22 April 2026 by European Commission.

card.x: Fifth version of the Manual on Borderline and Classification under MDR/IVDR. New sections on medicinal product administration devices. Updated EMDN FAQs on code extensions 90 vs 92.

proposals.reason: Digital Omnibus trilogue #2 scheduled for 28 April 2026, potentially finalizing AI Act high-risk deadline shifts for medical devices.

card.t: Digital Omnibus Trilogue #2 — AI Act Delay Decision
card.x: Second trilogue negotiation on the Digital Omnibus. May finalize: Annex III HR-AI delayed to Dec 2027, Annex I (medical devices) to Aug 2028. Parliament and Council positions confirmed Mar 2026.

proposals.reason: IMDRF opened public consultation on AI/ML Lifecycle Framework on 10 April 2026, closing 10 July 2026.

card.t: IMDRF AI/ML Lifecycle Framework — Consultation Closes
card.x: Public consultation deadline for the IMDRF draft Technical Framework for Artificial Intelligence Life Cycle Management. Key document for global AI medical device regulatory convergence.

proposals.reason: South Korea granted first approval for a generative AI medical device (chest X-ray analysis) in April 2026.

card.t: South Korea: First Generative AI Medical Device Approved
card.x: MFDS approved the first digital medical device powered by generative AI. Analyzes chest X-ray images and summarizes abnormal findings in text. Milestone beyond conventional image-analysis AI.

proposals.reason: G_NIUS launched Simplific'ACTION initiative on 21 April 2026 for digital health regulatory simplification in France.

card.x: French digital health agency ANS/G_NIUS launched Simplific'ACTION initiative to support digital health innovators with regulatory simplification. Also announced: EC call for AI medical imaging projects (22 Apr).

proposals.reason: MHRA developing Early Access Service for innovative AI medical devices, initially targeting dementia diagnostics, launching 2026.

card.t: MHRA Early Access Service — AI Diagnostics
card.x: Conditional market access pathway for innovative devices addressing unmet clinical needs. Initially targeting dementia diagnostics with 92% diagnostic target within 18 weeks. Relevant for AI-SaMD manufacturers.

proposals.reason: CNIL/HAS AI Healthcare Guide consultation is now closed (16 Apr). Final joint recommendation expected Q2 2026.

card.t: CNIL/HAS AI Healthcare Guide — Final Publication Expected
card.x: Joint recommendation on proper use of AI in healthcare for professionals and establishments. Consultation closed 16 Apr 2026. Final publication expected Q2 2026. Covers legal framework, governance, generative AI.

proposals.reason: EDPB adopted guidelines on scientific research and certification on 22 April 2026. Relevant for clinical evaluation data processing.

card.x: European Data Protection Board adopted guidelines on scientific research and certification. Relevant for medical device manufacturers processing clinical evaluation and PMS data under GDPR.
